codeReview.summary: The new guide is comprehensive, prose-first, and covers all required sections/examples with substantial code extracts, but it contains one blocking API-accuracy issue: it presents onConnectionValidate as an active lifecycle hook for connection acceptance/rejection even though current flow connection validation does not dispatch that hook in runtime code.

issues.description: The lifecycle hook example documents `onConnectionValidate` as a working extension point for allowing/rejecting connections, which is misleading in the current codebase. Frontend validation (`app/lib/flow/context.tsx` isValidConnection at lines 417-477) uses `canConnect/canCoerce` directly and does not call `dispatchHook('onConnectionValidate', ...)`; repository-wide usage is only in tests. This can mislead plugin authors into implementing a hook that has no effect.

sharedStateObservations.observation: docs-worker procedure says to verify API surfaces against actual implementation, but for hook docs this should explicitly require call-site validation (where hooks are dispatched), not just type/enum presence.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: docs/creating-plugins.md documents onConnectionValidate behavior; nodes/_types.ts and nodes/_types.py define the hook, but app/lib/flow/context.tsx isValidConnection (lines 417-477) does not dispatch it, and app/lib/flow grep shows dispatch usage only in tests.

summary: Reviewed feature plugin-authoring-docs (session 5ca817db-8316-48b2-9259-4938dd6df4df, commit ae9b827c4a9547d17f915e7ceb16ee493c7e14b0) against VAL-DOCS-001..008 and repo APIs. The document is high quality and complete in scope, but fails due to a blocking inaccuracy around onConnectionValidate being presented as functional when current runtime wiring does not invoke it.
